subject: some ideas for ye olde fish house
body:
Hi there,

85% of restaurant customers check the menu online before deciding where to eat.

I had some ideas about how Ye Olde Fish House could capture more of those people searching for food in Columbus.

1. Your 7,600 Facebook followers are a goldmine, but Facebook buries your menu behind ads and suggested restaurants. A simple website with your menu front and center means people see your food first, not your competitors.
2. Your fish sandwich and tenderloins are legendary -- but someone new to Columbus searching "best fish sandwich near me" can't find you. A site that shows up in that search would bring in customers who don't already know about you.
3. Your hours are tricky (Mon-Thu lunch only, Friday dinner). A website makes that crystal clear so people don't show up at the wrong time and leave frustrated.

Let me know if any of these resonate.

Eric Lefler
OphidianAI

P.S. Quick win you can do today -- your Google Business Profile probably has your menu from years ago. Updating it takes 10 minutes and it's the first thing people see when they Google you.
